_note: SOP build guides for the 16 to-be workflows. Grounded in data.json copy, wf-diagrams.json src, and the enum contract. 'BUILD DECISION NEEDED' marks values the spec does not yet fix. Regenerate with scripts/build_tobe_detail.py.

01.purpose: First touch. Capture every lead, stamp attribution onto the Opportunity at create, and run speed-to-lead outreach.

trigger.type: Form Submitted (and/or Inbound Webhook for paid funnels)
filters: Form is any of the MWC lead-capture forms
or Inbound Webhook from the funnel with gclid/fbclid/UTM in the payload
trigger.target: The live MWC lead forms. BUILD DECISION NEEDED: confirm the exact form ids / webhook to listen on.

prerequisites: Contact fields: gclid_value, fbclid_value, utm_source, utm_campaign, utm_medium, utm_content, utm_term, lead_source, lead_source_detail (Wave 0 creates these)

Wave 0 ADDENDUM (Decision 14): Locations Custom Object with 3 records (Richmond, Virginia Beach, Newport News) + generic operational contact fields current_clinic_address, current_clinic_phone, current_booking_url
Opportunity pipeline Sales - Lead to Close (id Vt8cPz51C3i87moo73gQ), New Lead stage
Opportunity attribution fields (utm_*, gclid, fbclid, lead_source) to receive the copy at create
D17 attribution fields exist (12 contact first_*/last_* + 5 frozen opportunity attr fields) -- see Decisions page

steps.name: Fields: Two-touch attribution capture (D17)
steps.config: Write inbound gclid/fbclid/utm_source/utm_campaign/landing_page to BOTH field sets per Decision 17: last_* fields overwrite on every inbound; first_* fields write ONLY IF EMPTY (use an If/Else 'first_utm_source is empty' gate so first-touch is write-once). Stamp first_touch_at/last_touch_at with {{right_now.middle_endian_date}}. UTM governance: if utm_source is not one of google/meta/gbp/bing/referral/direct, add tag attr_needs_review instead of silently storing.

steps.name: Fields: Set lead source
steps.config: Set contact.lead_source and contact.lead_source_detail. Confirm whether native contact.source is used instead (per as-is reference workflow).

steps.name: Opp: Create in New Lead
steps.config: Create Opportunity in Sales - Lead to Close (id Vt8cPz51C3i87moo73gQ), stage New Lead. Copy attribution onto the Opp at create: utm_source, utm_campaign, gclid, fbclid, lead_source. This is the single moment attribution lands on the Opp, so wins AND losses carry it. D17 FREEZE RULE: copy first_*/last_* attribution + gclid/fbclid/landing_page from contact onto the opportunity fields at CREATE, exactly once; no workflow may update opportunity attribution fields afterward.

steps.name: Email: Welcome (instant)
steps.config: Send immediately. Exempt from quiet hours (email, not SMS). Fires even at night.

steps.name: SMS: Speed-to-Lead (T+0, quiet-hours)
steps.config: Send instantly if inside 8a-9p contact TZ; if outside, the quiet-hours window queues it to 8:00 AM contact TZ (TCPA-safe).

steps.name: Notify: Staff new lead
steps.config: Internal notification to the setter team with lead + attribution summary.

steps.name: Call: Speed-to-Lead (T+5)
steps.config: Create a call task / auto-call to the setter team.

steps.name: Wait: 24 hours
steps.config: Wait 24 hours from entry to evaluate booking.

steps.name: If: Appointment booked within 24h?
steps.config: Branch on whether an appointment now exists for the contact.

branches.label: Yes - booked
branches.path: Exit to WF-03 Booking Confirmation and Reminders

messages.body: Subject: Welcome to Men's Wellness Centers. Body: Thanks for reaching out, {{contact.first_name}}. A Men's Wellness Centers team member will call you within one business hour to schedule your no-cost 60-minute in-person consultation at your local clinic. BUILD DECISION NEEDED: paste final approved welcome copy.

Hi {{contact.first_name}}, this is Men's Wellness Centers. Thanks for reaching out. What is the best time today for a quick call to book your no-cost 60-minute in-person consultation? Reply STOP to opt out.

settings.quiet_hours: Time Window 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM, Contact Timezone, all 7 days. Set under Workflow Settings, not per action.
settings.allow_reentry: No. A contact should run lead-capture once per lead. Re-entry would double-create opportunities.
settings.stop_on_response: Optional on the SMS nurture portion; not on the create/attribution steps.
settings.reentry_caveat: Fallback reconcile (form submissions vs opportunities every 15 min) catches any submission that did not create an Opportunity.
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Create a test contact via each lead form with fake gclid/UTM params in the URL.
Verify the Opportunity is created in New Lead with attribution copied onto the Opp (not just the contact).
Verify welcome email fires instantly and SMS respects quiet hours (submit a late-night test to confirm the SMS queues to 8 AM).
Leave it unbooked 24h and confirm exit to WF-02; book within 24h and confirm exit to WF-03.
depends_on: WF-02 and WF-03 to receive exits

variables.principle: Build ONE workflow, not one per clinic. Location is a static variable set once at object creation (WF-01 branches on opportunity.location and stamps the clinic-specific fields). Every downstream step reads merge fields, so the same workflow serves Richmond, Virginia Beach, and Newport News. Never hardcode a clinic name, address, phone, or booking URL into a message or action; never clone a workflow just to change words. Branch per location only when BEHAVIOR differs (a different calendar or routing), never when only the copy differs.
variables.location_variable: opportunity.location (static: richmond | va_beach | npn | telehealth). Set once by WF-01 at create. This is the single source of clinic identity; downstream workflows read it, they do not re-derive it.
variables.collision_warning: CRITICAL: a standard GHL Custom Value is account-wide. It applies universally across the whole sub-account, so it CANNOT hold per-clinic data. If one location writes a Custom Value it changes for every location (a data collision). Custom Values are only safe for values that are identical brand-wide (brand name, main brand domain, compliance links). Per-clinic values must NEVER live in a Custom Value.

methods.name: Method 1 - Locations Custom Object (LOCKED: Decision 14)
methods.detail: The chosen pattern. A 'Locations' Custom Object with one record per clinic (fields: Location Email, Location Phone, Booking Link, Address, Review Link). Each Contact is associated with its Location at capture, and WF-01 stamps the associated Location's values into generic operational contact fields (current_clinic_address, current_clinic_phone, current_booking_url) exactly once at creation. Because email/SMS builders cannot loop Custom Object variables natively, that stamp step is the bridge; every template reads ONLY the generic fields, so downstream workflows never know clinics exist. Wave 0 addendum: the object + 3 records + generic fields must be created (Wave 0 ran before Decision 14).

methods.name: Method 2 - Contact field matrix (REJECTED)
methods.detail: Prefixed per-clinic fields plus tag-routed If/Else copying into generic fields. Rejected by Decision 14 because the clinic values end up embedded inside workflow branch configs, which buries data in steps (the defect this project removes) and makes every clinic-data change a workflow edit.

methods.name: Method 3 - Smart URL strategy (ADOPTED for links)
methods.detail: Layered on per Decision 14 for booking/review links on the Vercel funnel pages: one base Custom Value plus the contact's location as a URL parameter, for example {{custom_values.base_booking_url}}?branch={{contact.location_preference}}. The landing page reads ?branch= and renders the correct clinic calendar. Links only; in-message address/phone comes from the generic fields.

custom_values: Safe as account-wide Custom Values ({{custom_values.name}}): brand name, main brand booking domain, compliance links, brand-level review/survey/intake links. These are identical for every contact and every clinic.

NOT safe as Custom Values (per-clinic, would collide): clinic address, clinic direct phone, per-clinic booking/calendar URL, location email. Deliver these via Method 1 or 2: copy the clinic's value into a generic operational Contact field with an Update Contact Field step before the send, then reference that generic field.

Merge-field syntax: contact fields {{contact.field_key}}, opportunity fields {{opportunity.field_key}}, account constants {{custom_values.name}}, appointment {{appointment.start_time}}. Operational pattern: templates read a generic field like {{contact.current_location_booking_link}} that the workflow fills per clinic, never a hardcoded clinic value.

02.purpose: Recover leads that never booked: a 10-touch sequence over 16 days, exit instantly on booking.

trigger.type: Contact Tag or workflow hand-off from WF-01
filters: Entered when WF-01 finds no booking 24h after the lead
trigger.target: Hand-off from WF-01 (no separate GHL trigger needed if WF-01 adds to this workflow).

prerequisites: WF-09 built to receive the 16-day exit

steps.name: Call: Speed-to-Lead (T+5m)
steps.config: Call task, 5 minutes from no-book entry.

steps.name: SMS: Re-Engagement 2 (T+1d)
steps.config: Appointment/consult-logistics framing, no symptom detail. Quiet hours apply.

steps.name: Exit: to WF-09
steps.config: After 16 days hand off to WF-09 Long-Term Nurture.

messages.body: Hi {{contact.first_name}}, following up from Men's Wellness Centers. Ready to book your no-cost 60-minute in-person consultation? Reply with a day that works. Reply STOP to opt out.
{{contact.first_name}}, we will step back for now. When you are ready to book your consultation at Men's Wellness Centers, we are here. Reply STOP to opt out.

settings.quiet_hours: Time Window 8:00 AM to 9:00 PM, Contact Timezone, all 7 days. Set under Workflow Settings, not per action.
settings.allow_reentry: No while active; the sequence is one pass per lead.
settings.stop_on_response: Yes - a reply should pause automated sends for setter handling.
settings.reentry_caveat: Booking at any point triggers the early exit below.
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Enter a test contact from WF-01 with no booking.
Verify each touch fires on schedule and inside quiet hours.
Book mid-sequence and confirm immediate exit to WF-03.
Let it run 16 days and confirm hand-off to WF-09.

03.purpose: Confirm the booking and run all pre-appointment reminders on the new class calendars.

trigger.type: Appointment (Customer booked appointment) on the class calendars
filters: Calendar is one of the 3 per-clinic class calendars
trigger.target: BUILD DECISION NEEDED: the 3 new per-clinic class calendar ids (Richmond, Virginia Beach, Newport News), created in Wave 0. 60-min slots, 60-min interval, 6 seats per slot, 'Show seats per slot' OFF (Decision 12).

branches.label: Yes
branches.path: Skip chase, go to Reminders block

branches.label: No
branches.path: Run intake chase (WF-04 owns chasing; see WF-04)

steps.name: Email: 3-Day Prep (T-3d)
steps.config: 3 days before appointment. Use appointment-relative wait with 'if date already passed' fallback.

steps.name: SMS: Morning-Of (8 AM)
steps.config: 8 AM on the day of the appointment.

steps.name: Exit: appointment day
steps.config: On appointment day WF-05 fires from the PCC Sales Form. A cancellation at any point halts reminders and routes to WF-08.

steps.name: Port: Auto-confirm + recovery exit (live 3a)
steps.config: On booking: update_appointment_status=confirmed (when auto-confirm rules met), remove_from_workflow [WF-02 Non-Booked Recovery], remove stale recovery opportunity if one exists in No-Show/Cancel limbo. Node shapes in build_ids live_node_shapes_port_pass.

steps.name: Port: Reply-based confirm handler (live 03c)
steps.config: Inbound SMS reply parsing on unconfirmed appointments: C/CONFIRM -> update_appointment_status=confirmed + remove from confirmation chase; R -> reschedule path. Port the 03c pattern as a WF-03 reply branch, not a separate workflow.

filters: Intake form not completed after the T+1h reminder

05.purpose: THE KEYSTONE. Route on the outcome the PCC Sales Form wrote. WF-05 never writes outcomes.

trigger.type: Opportunity or Contact update from the PCC Sales Form submission
filters: Fires when the PCC Sales Form is submitted (it is the sole writer of sale_outcome, sale_type, appt_status, value)
trigger.target: The PCC Sales Form / the Opportunity record the PCC edits.

prerequisites: PCC Sales Form live and writing sale_outcome (sold|ad|mut|mar), sale_type (new|renewal), appt_status (showed|no-show|cancel|reschedule), value, term_1
Sales - Lead to Close (id Vt8cPz51C3i87moo73gQ) with Won / A&D / MUT stages
WF-06, WF-07, WF-08, WF-09, WF-13 built to receive exits

branches.label: no-show or cancel
branches.path: Exit to WF-08 No-Show and Cancel Recovery

branches.label: reschedule
branches.path: Exit to WF-03 Booking Confirmation and Reminders

branches.label: Yes
branches.path: Update fields only. No re-route, no CAPI. End.

branches.label: ad
branches.path: Move Opp to Lost, ad_reason already on Opp, stamp outcome_processed_at, tag ad + outcome_ad, exit to WF-07

branches.label: mut
branches.path: Move Opp to Lost - MUT, stamp outcome_processed_at, tag mut + outcome_mut, suppress future automation via WF-11

branches.label: mar
branches.path: Keep Opp Open, open a PCC/provider task, tag outcome_mar. On approval the form is resubmitted with a final outcome and WF-05 re-runs.

steps.name: Opp: Set Won
steps.config: Sold branch: Move Opp to Won. monetary_value = net collected. Require sale_type (new|renewal) from the form. Set renewal_date = Won date + term_1. Stamp outcome_processed_at. Tag sold + outcome_sold.

steps.name: Wait: 60 seconds
steps.config: Let value commit before the ad conversion reads it (read-after-write race).

steps.name: Webhook: Signal WF-13 once
steps.config: Signal WF-13 Ad Conversions once (Booked + Won) to Google and Meta. Guarded by outcome_processed_at so it fires once per opportunity.

branches.label: new
branches.path: Exit to WF-06 Post-Visit Won and Onboarding

branches.label: renewal
branches.path: Exit to WF-09 Renewal Reminders sub-flow

steps.name: Port: find_opportunity guard (before outcome writes)
steps.config: Use find_opportunity (sorting: latest, multipath Opportunity Found / Not Found) before outcome writes so updates target the correct opportunity instead of assuming trigger context. Live shape from 'After Appointment Feedback Survey Send'.

messages.body: WF-05 sends no member messages. It only routes. A 'no form by end of appointment day' chaser nags the PCC internally and writes no outcome.

settings.allow_reentry: Yes - must re-run on form resubmission (MAR approval path), but the outcome_processed_at stamp prevents duplicate routing/CAPI.
settings.reentry_caveat: An edit or duplicate submit updates fields but never re-fires routing or the ad conversion (idempotency).
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off. This is the P0 fix: remove auto-Won (Decision 10).

test: Submit the PCC form as showed+sold+new: confirm Won, renewal_date set, tag sold, CAPI fires once, exit to WF-06.
Submit as no-show: confirm routes to WF-08, no outcome written.
Resubmit the same sold form: confirm fields update but no second CAPI (outcome_processed_at held).
Submit as mar: confirm Opp stays Open and a PCC task opens.

11.purpose: The single DND and consent authority. Every send checks it. Also handles bounces and MUT suppression.

trigger.type: Consulted by every messaging workflow; plus event triggers for DNC, Email Bounce, MUT
filters: sms_consent_status = opted_in AND not DND AND inside 8a-9p contact TZ

prerequisites: audit trail / Google Sheet for bounces

steps.name: If: Send gate
steps.config: For every SMS/voicemail send: allow only if sms_consent_status = opted_in AND not DND AND inside 8a-9p contact TZ; otherwise hold.

steps.name: DNC: Set DND + halt
steps.config: On DNC flag: set DND on the contact and halt all automation.

steps.name: Bounce: Remove from email
steps.config: On email bounce: remove from email sends and log to the audit trail.

steps.name: MUT: Suppress automation
steps.config: On MUT outcome: suppress all future automation for the contact.

steps.name: Port: email bounce handler (live 3. Email Bounce)
steps.config: On email bounce: dnd_contact (Email channel only) + tag email_bounced + internal notification. Port as a WF-11 branch.

steps.name: Port: DNC/wrong-number/error hygiene (live 2. DNC)
steps.config: Port the live error-hygiene trigger set at go-live: invalid-number -> dnd Call+SMS + tag bad_number; opt-out keyword set beyond STOP (remove/unsubscribe/wrong person) -> dnd SMS; SMS delivery errors -> notify. 10 trigger patterns exist live; consolidate to 3-4 canonical ones.

messages.body: WF-11 sends nothing. It gates and suppresses. Assume-consent for the 11,077 total historical contacts is an accepted risk (Decision 8).

settings.quiet_hours: This workflow enforces quiet hours for all others.
settings.reentry_caveat: Every other messaging workflow must filter on this gate rather than re-implementing consent logic.
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Set a test contact to DND and confirm no sends go out.
Flag a bounce and confirm email removal + audit log.
Set MUT and confirm all automation suppresses.

12.purpose: Handle call dispositions; write opportunity.call_disposition; route negative-NPS follow-ups. Never writes sale fields.

trigger.type: Call ends on softphone / disposition workflow fires

prerequisites: patient_care_consultant_id (written only by the PCC Sales Form)
Channel-level tracking numbers provisioned (Google Ads / GBP / site / other) so dialed number maps to channel (D17)

branches.label: Bad number
branches.path: Write call_disposition = bad_number, flag for data cleanup

branches.label: Yes
branches.path: Assign task to owning PCC for a manual follow-up call

steps.name: Attr: stamp phone lead source (D17)
steps.config: On call disposition, if the contact has no first_utm_source, set first_utm_source=phone and last_utm_source=phone, and stamp the dialed tracking number into last_landing_page (or a dedicated dialed_number field). Ensures called-in leads are not attribution-blank.

messages.body: WF-12 never writes sale_outcome, sale_type, or value. That is the PCC Sales Form only (H5 single-writer guardrail). It only reads patient_care_consultant_id.

settings.reentry_caveat: Reads but never writes the PCC id; do not let it write sale fields.
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Fire each disposition and confirm call_disposition writes correctly.
Flag a negative-NPS from WF-06 and confirm a PCC task is created.
Confirm no sale fields are ever written by this workflow.

variables.collision_warning: CRITICAL: a standard GHL Custom Value is account-wide. It applies universally across the whole sub-account, so it CANNOT hold per-clinic data. If one location writes a Custom Value it changes for every location (a data collision). methods.name: Method 1 - Locations Custom Object (LOCKED: Decision 14)
methods.detail: The chosen pattern. A 'Locations' Custom Object with one record per clinic (fields: Location Email, Location Phone, Booking Link, Address, Review Link). Each Contact is associated with its Location at capture, and WF-01 stamps the associated Location's values into generic operational contact fields (current_clinic_address, current_clinic_phone, current_booking_url) exactly once at creation. Wave 0 addendum: the object + 3 records + generic fields must be created (Wave 0 ran before Decision 14).

methods.name: Method 2 - Contact field matrix (REJECTED)
methods.detail: Prefixed per-clinic fields plus tag-routed If/Else copying into generic fields. Rejected by Decision 14 because the clinic values end up embedded inside workflow branch configs, which buries data in steps (the defect this project removes) and makes every clinic-data change a workflow edit.

methods.name: Method 3 - Smart URL strategy (ADOPTED for links)
methods.detail: Layered on per Decision 14 for booking/review links on the Vercel funnel pages: one base Custom Value plus the contact's location as a URL parameter, for example {{custom_values.base_booking_url}}?branch={{contact.location_preference}}. The landing page reads ?branch= and renders the correct clinic calendar. Links only; in-message address/phone comes from the generic fields.

custom_values: Safe as account-wide Custom Values ({{custom_values.name}}): brand name, main brand booking domain, compliance links, brand-level review/survey/intake links. These are identical for every contact and every clinic.

13.purpose: Fire Booked and Won conversions to Google and Meta. Exclude A&D. Do not key off ad_* fields. D17: closed-loop value feedback enables value-based bidding (optimize for men who buy, not form-fills).

trigger.type: Opportunity Booked event and Opportunity Won event (signaled by WF-05)

prerequisites: Native facebook_conversion_api + google_adword conversion actions (exist in account per local-session scan)
Frozen opportunity attribution fields populated by WF-01 (D17)

steps.name: Conv: Showed event (D17)
steps.config: Fire on appt_status=showed. Higher-volume optimization signal; lets campaigns be judged on show rate weeks before revenue. Payload includes gclid (frozen opp field) + event_id=opportunity ID.

steps.name: Conv: Won with revenue value (D17)
steps.config: Fire once per opportunity on Won (gate on outcome_processed_at). Send actual revenue value; SEPARATE conversion actions for sale_type=new vs renewal. gclid/fbclid from FROZEN opportunity fields; event_id=opportunity ID for pixel/server dedup. If click is outside the 90-day window (renewals), use enhanced conversions (hashed email/phone) instead of click ID.

messages.body: GUARDRAIL: sale_outcome's AD value and the ad_reason field are named for 'Advise and Decline', NOT advertising. Do not key conversion logic off ad_* fields or tags, or lost consultations will be reported as fake conversions to Meta and Google.

settings.reentry_caveat: Fire once per opportunity; the WF-05 idempotency stamp prevents duplicate Won conversions.
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Book a test opportunity and confirm one Booked conversion.
Win it and confirm one Won conversion.
Set A&D and confirm NO conversion fires.
Resubmit the form and confirm no duplicate Won conversion.

16.purpose: Missed-call text-back plus a simultaneous-ring IVR path and chat out-of-office auto-response.

steps.name: IVR: Simultaneous ring
steps.config: Ring all locations at once (absorbs '1. IVR - simultaneous').

steps.name: SMS: Instant Text-Back (T+0)
steps.config: Immediate text-back on a missed call.

steps.name: Chat: Auto-Response
steps.config: On chat OOO, auto-respond with the scheduling link.

steps.name: Attr: missed-call source stamp (D17)
steps.config: On missed call, apply the same phone-source stamping as WF-12: first_utm_source=phone if empty, last_utm_source=phone, dialed tracking number captured. Number-pool DNI upgrade path documented in Decision 17.

steps.name: Port: IVR tree (live 1. IVR - simultaneous)
steps.config: ivr_say greeting -> ivr_gather (press 1 new consult / 2 existing / 3 billing) -> ivr_connect_call (simultaneous ring to clinic user list, record-from-answer) -> ivr_collect_voicemail fallback. All 4 node shapes captured in build_ids.

steps.name: Port: business-hours gate + chat-widget OOO (live Chat Widget OOO)
steps.config: The live workflow proves a NATIVE business-hours condition: if_else on quarter_hour >= open AND quarter_hour <= close AND day_week in Mon-Fri. Port as WF-16's hours gate for missed-call/chat-widget auto-replies (within hours: instant text-back; outside: OOO message with booking link). Also covers chat-widget inquiries, which the 16-workflow set previously did not handle at all.

messages.body: Sorry we missed your call to Men's Wellness Centers, {{contact.first_name}}. How can we help? You can book your no-cost 60-minute in-person consultation here: {{custom_values.booking_link}} Reply STOP to opt out.

settings.quiet_hours: 8a-9p contact TZ on outbound sends (WF-11 gate).
settings.reentry_caveat: IVR simultaneous-ring is the intended path (absorbs the legacy IVR workflow).
settings.status: Draft. Do not publish without sign-off.

test: Place a test call and miss it; confirm instant text-back + 30-min follow-up.
Confirm simultaneous ring across locations.
Trigger chat OOO and confirm the auto-response with scheduling link.

17.purpose: PCC consult pricing tool (ported from live 06. Price Calculator). During the consult, PCC selects program options on the contact/opportunity; math_operation nodes compute the package total into a total-price field and log a structured note. Feeds the monetary value that WF-13's Won-with-revenue conversion sends to ad platforms (D17).
17.trigger: Contact tag price_calc OR PCC form submission (defer until go-live pass, like all triggers)
prerequisites: Price component fields exist (live: z0nsXjBpmFXGdZGfzHAI, rGf0p5eaxAWTM9y5KaAE -> total cxUGCjBinbuh0spb4LQS -- audit and map to canonical names)

steps.name: Gate: which program selected
steps.config: Branch on program selection field (live gate: cClk7ad4PHxiake2lChS)

steps.name: Compute: package total
steps.config: math_operation numerical: selectField (component price) -> operators [add ...] -> updateField (total). Live shape in build_ids live_node_shapes_port_pass.

steps.name: Note: structured price quote
steps.config: Write the computed quote as a structured contact note for the PCC record

17.test: Set component fields on a test contact, run, verify total field arithmetic and note content
depends_on: WF-13 (consumes total as Won revenue value)
